assertion_results.text: Output must mention CI run time of 47 minutes
assertion_results.evidence: Multiple mentions: executive summary ('47-minute Jenkins build'), area scores table ('47-minute build, no clear gate'), and top blockers ('47-minute Jenkins build — feedback is too slow to be actionable').

assertion_results.text: Output must address flaky tests specifically (not just 'tests exist')
assertion_results.evidence: Top blocker #1: '30% (~3,600 tests) are unreliable and ignored, making the entire suite untrustworthy; developers cannot get honest signal from CI.' Specific count, percentage, and impact are all stated.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Short term (this sprint)' section lists four concrete actions, and the action items table marks A1–A4 with horizon 'short' (e.g. 'Quarantine all flaky tests and open per-team flakiness backlog').

assertion_results.text: Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Area scores table explicitly shows: 'CI/CD | 2/5 | Jenkins from 2011, 47-minute build, no clear gate'.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend replacing Jenkins without acknowledging migration cost
assertion_results.evidence: Jenkins replacement is deferred to long-term and explicitly qualified: 'Evaluate Jenkins modernization … but only after the higher-urgency items above are stabilized.' Migration is framed as 'institutional risk' and an 'investment', acknowledging cost and sequencing constraints.
